According to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', the Empire was [[Declaration of a New Order|founded]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], while ''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates ''Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale'' to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], thereby establishing the [[Time/Legends|time]] period of Dyll's death.</ref>

{{Quote|<nowiki></nowiki>''Music''! You uncivilized barbarian! ''Music''! I have the music of Maxa Jandovar, and Orin Mersai. M'lar'Nkai'kambric. ''Lubrics'', ''Aishara'', ''Dyll''—|Kardue'sai'Malloc, to Boba Fett|<ref name="tlosttobf">{{StoryCite|story=The Last One Standing: The Tale of Boba Fett|book=Tales of the Bounty Hunters}}</ref>}}
'''Brullian Dyll''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Musician/Legends|musician]] who was deemed to be a threat to the stability of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which ruled much of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during his life. Despite the renown of his work, Dyll was [[Death/Legends|executed]] by Imperial authorities, and his music was suppressed. Only many [[Standard year/Legends|years]] later, following the rise of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], would a chance come for Dyll's works to be heard once again.

==Biography==
A [[Musician/Legends|musician]], Brullian Dyll was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] who lived during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="cswe" /> an authoritarian government that rose to power in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="tnec">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> Dyll's work, which was performed in concerts, came to be highly regarded, and he was perceived as a legendary [[Music/Legends|musician]].<ref name="tlosttobf" /><ref name="ebtdt">{{StoryCite|story=Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}</ref> The Empire, however, was a censorial regime and began systematically hunting down, [[Prisoner/Legends|imprisoning]], and [[Death/Legends|executing]] many musicians and confiscating their music.<ref name="ebtdt" /> Dyll became a victim of one of these purges<ref name="cswe" /> by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="Date" /> as his [[Artist/Legends|artistic]] endeavors were deemed "questionable" by the Imperial authorities.<ref name="cswe" /> Following the musician's death, records of his music were also purged.<ref name="tlosttobf" />

Despite the Empire's success in suppressing Dyll's music, recordings of the musician's work eventually came into the possession of [[Kardue'sai'Malloc/Legends|Kardue'sai'Malloc]], a fugitive [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] war criminal with a predilection for music. Once Malloc was finally caught by the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]]<ref name="tlosttobf" /> in [[19 ABY/Legends|19 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> during the reign of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], the Devaronian made his captor promise to sell the music to a contact so that it could be distributed throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] once again.<ref name="tlosttobf" />

==Personality and traits==
Dyll was a skilled and celebrated musician whose work was deemed to be a threat to the authoritarian regime of the Galactic Empire.<ref name="tlosttobf" /><ref name="ebtdt" />

==Behind the scenes==
Brullian Dyll was first mentioned in ''[[Empire Blues: The Devaronian's Tale]]'', a short story written by [[Daniel Keys Moran]] for the [[1995]] anthology book ''[[Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina]]''.<ref name="ebtdt" />
